idea-plugin.name: FHIR Toolkit
idea-plugin.description:
FHIR Toolkit — productivity tooling for healthcare developers working with FHIR R4.

Inline validation, visual resource exploration, multi-resource scenario generation, an embedded mock server, and an interactive FHIRPath playground — all without leaving your IDE.

Features

  • Inline validation — HAPI FHIR R4 validator surfaces issues as you type, with FHIRPath-resolved error locations.
  • FHIR Resource tree tool window — collapsible visual hierarchy of the active resource; click any node to navigate to that JSON element.
  • Bundle Graph tool window — each entry shown with its outgoing references, marked ✓ resolved or ✗ unresolved within the Bundle. Click to navigate.
  • FHIRPath playground — interactive query input + result panel; evaluates FHIRPath expressions against the open resource using HAPI's engine.
  • Mock FHIR server — Tools → Run Mock FHIR Server spins up an in-memory R4 endpoint on a localhost port for client wiring tests.
  • Sample resource generator — insert minimum-valid skeletons for Patient, Observation, Encounter, Bundle, Practitioner, Organization.
  • Insert FHIR Scenario — pick from 4 hand-crafted multi-resource clinical Bundles (diabetes, pediatric asthma, hypertension+lipid panel, acute MI).
  • Datatype hovers — contextual documentation for CodeableConcept, Coding, Reference, Quantity, Identifier properties.
  • Bundle reference navigation — gutter icons next to reference values jump to the matching entry.
  • Curated code lookup — ~380 hover-readable codes across LOINC, SNOMED CT, ICD-10-CM, and UCUM.

Designed for engineers building EHR integrations, healthtech apps, and payer systems. Open source, no telemetry, no outbound network calls. The optional Mock FHIR Server binds to 127.0.0.1 only.

action.description: Insert a minimum-valid FHIR R4 resource skeleton at cursor
Start an in-memory mock FHIR R4 server on a local port for client testing
Insert a multi-resource clinical scenario Bundle at cursor (diabetes, asthma, hypertension, MI, etc.)
